title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: members, contributors, leaders pledge make participation community harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, caste, color, religion, sexual identity orientation. pledge act interact ways contribute open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, healthy community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders responsible clarifying enforcing standards acceptable behavior take appropriate fair corrective action response behavior deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, harmful. Community leaders right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct, communicate reasons moderation decisions appropriate.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported community leaders responsible enforcement c.martinez0@outlook.com. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly. community leaders obligated respect privacy security reporter incident.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Use inappropriate language behavior deemed unprofessional unwelcome community. Consequence: private, written warning community leaders, providing clarity around nature violation explanation behavior inappropriate. public apology may requested.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: violation single incident series actions. title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant, version 2.1, available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct.html. Community Impact Guidelines inspired [Mozilla’s code conduct enforcement ladder][https://github.com/mozilla/inclusion]. answers common questions code conduct, see FAQ https://www.contributor-covenant.org/faq. title: Getting Started with laOpenData
text: Welcome laOpenData package, R package dedicated helping R users connect Los Angeles Open Data Portal! laOpenData package provides streamlined interface accessing Los Angeles’ vast open data resources. connects directly official City Los Angeles open data portals, including datasets hosted across Socrata-powered city domains, helping users bridge gap raw city APIs tidy data analysis. package part broader ecosystem open data tools designed provide consistent interface across cities. two ways:

title: Getting Started with laOpenData
text: primary way pull data package la_pull_dataset() function, works tandem la_list_datasets(). need know anything API keys authentication. first step call la_list_datasets() see datasets list available use la_pull_dataset() function. provides information thousands datasets found portal. output includes columns dataset title, description, link source. important fields dataset key id. need either order use la_pull_dataset() function. can put either key value id value dataset = filter inside la_pull_dataset(). instance, want pull dataset Building Safety - Vacant Building Abatement, can use either methods : matter put id key value dataset =, successfully get data!
code: la_list_datasets() |> head() #> # A tibble: 6 × 96 #>   key   id    name  attribution attributionLink category createdAt dataUpdatedAt #>   <chr> <chr> <chr> <chr>       <chr>           <chr>    <chr>     <chr>         #> 1 my_l… 2cy6… MyLA… City of Lo… https://myla31… City In… 2026-01-… 2026-04-13T0… #> 2 city… rwwd… City… Youth Deve… https://ydd.la… NA       2026-01-… 2026-01-09T2… #> 3 lahd… cr8f… LAHD… Los Angele… NA              Communi… 2025-11-… 2025-12-29T1… #> 4 x202… mt59… 2025… NA          NA              Housing… 2025-09-… 2026-01-27T2… #> 5 lahd… ci3m… LAHD… NA          NA              Communi… 2025-09-… 2025-09-11T1… #> 6 lahd… n9x9… LAHD… Los Angele… NA              Communi… 2025-09-… 2025-09-11T2… #> # ℹ 88 more variables: dataUri <chr>, description <chr>, domain <chr>, #> #   externalId <lgl>, hideFromCatalog <lgl>, hideFromDataJson <lgl>, #> #   license <chr>, metadataUpdatedAt <chr>, provenance <chr>, updatedAt <chr>, #> #   webUri <chr>, approvals <list>, tags <list>, #> #   `customFields.Committed Update Frequency.Refresh rate` <chr>, #> #   `customFields.Location Specified.Does this data have a Location column? (Yes or No)` <chr>, #> #   `customFields.Location Specified.What geographic unit is the data collected?` <chr>, … la_building_safety_vacant <- la_pull_dataset(   dataset = "q3ak-s5hy", limit = 2, timeout_sec = 90)  la_building_safety_vacant <- la_pull_dataset(   dataset = "building_and_safety_vacant_building_abatement", limit = 2, timeout_sec = 90)

title: Getting Started with laOpenData
text: easiest workflow use la_list_datasets() together la_pull_dataset(). event particular dataset want use R list, can use la_any_dataset(). requirement dataset’s API endpoint (URL provided Los Angeles Open Data portal). steps get : Los Angeles Open Data Portal, go dataset want work . Click “Export” (next actions button right hand side). Click “API Endpoint”. Click “SODA2” “Version”. Copy API Endpoint. example use la_any_dataset() API endpoint discovered, pull data la_pull_dataset() example:

title: Getting Started with laOpenData
text: functions provide access Los Angeles Open Data, serve slightly different purposes. general: Use la_pull_dataset() dataset available la_list_datasets() Use la_any_dataset() working datasets outside catalog Together, functions allow users either quickly access datasets flexibly query dataset available Los Angeles Open Data portal.

title: Getting Started with laOpenData
text: Los Angeles lot people, just many businesses, list active businesses LA contained dataset, found . R, laOpenData package can used pull data directly. using la_pull_dataset() function, can gather information businesses, filter based upon columns inside dataset. Let’s take example 3 requests occur actual city Los Angeles. la_pull_dataset() function can filter based columns dataset. filter, add filters = list() put whatever filters like inside. colnames call , know column called “city” can use accomplish . Success! calling la_businesses dataset see 3 rows data, distinct() call see location featured dataset LOS ANGELES. One strongest qualities function ability filter based multiple columns. Let’s put everything together get dataset 50 businesses occur LOS ANGELES council district 8. successfully created dataset contains 50 requests regarding businesses district 8 LA.
code: la_businesses <- la_pull_dataset(dataset = "6rrh-rzua",limit = 3, timeout_sec = 90, filters = list(city = "LOS ANGELES")) la_businesses #> # A tibble: 3 × 16 #>   location_account  business_name              street_address     city  zip_code #>   <chr>             <chr>                      <chr>              <chr> <chr>    #> 1 0003301266-0001-1 WEST OF HOLLYWOOD, INC.    2608 AIKEN AVENUE  LOS … 90064-3… #> 2 0002772430-0001-9 GISELLE LUZA STUDIO, LLC   5500 HOLLYWOOD BL… LOS … 90028-6… #> 3 0002173704-0001-5 ATLANTIC RECOVERY SERVICES 5306 N FIGUEROA S… LOS … 90042-4… #> # ℹ 11 more variables: location_description <chr>, mailing_address <chr>, #> #   mailing_city <chr>, mailing_zip_code <chr>, naics <dbl>, #> #   primary_naics_description <chr>, council_district <dbl>, #> #   location_start_date <dttm>, location_1_latitude <dbl>, #> #   location_1_longitude <dbl>, location_1_human_address <chr>  # Checking to see the filtering worked la_businesses |>   distinct(city) #> # A tibble: 1 × 1 #>   city        #>   <chr>       #> 1 LOS ANGELES # Creating the dataset la_businesses_8 <- la_pull_dataset(dataset = "6rrh-rzua", limit = 50, timeout_sec = 90, filters = list(city = "LOS ANGELES", council_district = 8))  # Calling head of our new dataset la_businesses_8 |>   slice_head(n = 6) #> # A tibble: 6 × 17 #>   location_account  business_name                 street_address  city  zip_code #>   <chr>             <chr>                         <chr>           <chr> <chr>    #> 1 0002810621-0001-9 NANCY'S CLEANING SERVICES     1742 W 64TH ST… LOS … 90047-1… #> 2 0003318926-0001-1 RINKA SHIRAISHI               4214 W 62ND ST… LOS … 90043-3… #> 3 0002824469-0001-9 JOSE CRUZ                     3114 W 59TH ST… LOS … 90043-3… #> 4 0003170618-0001-1 BULLHEAD CITY INN CORPORATION 10918 S FIGUER… LOS … 90061-1… #> 5 0002859001-0001-3 ASIA HAMILTON                 8953 RUTHELEN … LOS … 90047-3… #> 6 0002482305-0001-2 LISA M NIXON                  6106 KENISTON … LOS … 90043-3… #> # ℹ 12 more variables: location_description <chr>, mailing_address <chr>, #> #   mailing_city <chr>, mailing_zip_code <chr>, council_district <dbl>, #> #   location_start_date <dttm>, dba_name <chr>, naics <dbl>, #> #   primary_naics_description <chr>, location_1_latitude <dbl>, #> #   location_1_longitude <dbl>, location_1_human_address <chr>  # Quick check to make sure our filtering worked la_businesses_8 |>   summarize(rows = n()) #> # A tibble: 1 × 1 #>    rows #>   <int> #> 1    50  la_businesses_8 |>   distinct(city) #> # A tibble: 1 × 1 #>   city        #>   <chr>       #> 1 LOS ANGELES  la_businesses_8 |>   distinct(council_district) #> # A tibble: 1 × 1 #>   council_district #>              <dbl> #> 1                8

title: Getting Started with laOpenData
text: Now successfully pulled data R, let’s mini analysis using primary_naics_description column, figure main types businesses , create bar graph business types. Bar chart showing frequency business types LA district 8. graph shows us businesses area, many .
code: # Visualizing the distribution, ordered by frequency la_businesses_8 |>   count(primary_naics_description) |>   ggplot(aes(     x = n,     y = reorder(primary_naics_description, n)   )) +   geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +   theme_minimal() +   labs(     title = "Top 50 Business Types in District 8 of LA",     x = "Number of Businesses",     y = "Business Type"   )

title: Getting Started with laOpenData
text: laOpenData package serves robust interface Los Angeles Open Data portal, streamlining path raw city APIs actionable insights. abstracting complexities data acquisition—pagination, type-casting, complex filtering—allows users focus analysis rather data engineering. demonstrated vignette, package provides seamless workflow targeted data retrieval, automated filtering, rapid visualization.

what: Convenient Access to Los Angeles Open Data API Endpoints
title: Convenient Access to Los Angeles Open Data API Endpoints
text: laOpenData provides simple, reproducible access Los Angeles-related datasets theLos Angeles Open Data Portal platform — directly R, API keys manual downloads required. Working directly Socrata APIs can cumbersome — laOpenData simplifies process clean, reproducible workflow. Version 0.1.0 introduces streamlined, catalog-driven interface Los Angeles Open Data, extending open data ecosystem beyond New York support cross-city analysis comparative civic research. package provides three core functions: la_list_datasets() — Browse available datasets live Los Angeles Open Data catalog la_pull_dataset() — Pull cataloged dataset key, filtering, ordering, optional date controls la_any_dataset() — Pull Los Angeles Open Data dataset directly via Socrata JSON endpoint Datasets pulled via la_pull_dataset() automatically apply sensible defaults catalog (default ordering date fields), still allowing user control : limit filters date / / order clean_names coerce_types redesign reduces maintenance burden, improves extensibility, provides scalable interface working Los Angeles Open Data. functions return clean tibble outputs support filtering viafilters = list(field = "value").

title: Convenient Access to Los Angeles Open Data API Endpoints
Convenient Access to Los Angeles Open Data API Endpoints
code: library(laOpenData)  la_vacent_buildings <- la_pull_dataset(   dataset = "building_and_safety_vacant_building_abatement",   limit = 1000 )  head(la_vacent_buildings) #> # A tibble: 6 × 10 #>   address         case_num    cd inspector_name area  building_type approved_use #>   <chr>              <dbl> <dbl> <chr>          <chr> <chr>         <chr>        #> 1 749 S KOHLER ST  1045424    14 LUCIANO GAUNA  II    III           COMMERCIAL   #> 2 11354 W RUNNYM…  1044681     2 GLEN RAND      I     *****         <NA>         #> 3 1346 W 5TH ST     924823     1 LUCIANO GAUNA  II    *****         COMMERCIAL   #> 4 11354 W RUNNYM…  1044681     2 GLEN RAND      I     *****         <NA>         #> 5 11354 W RUNNYM…  1044681     2 GLEN RAND      I     *****         <NA>         #> 6 2219 S CENTRAL…  1026127     9 LUCIANO GAUNA  II    *****         <NA>         #> # ℹ 3 more variables: assigned_to <chr>, building_size <chr>, #> #   abate_effective <dttm>

title: Convenient Access to Los Angeles Open Data API Endpoints
text: laOpenData makes Los Angeles’s civic datasets accessible students, educators, analysts, researchers unified user-friendly R interface. Developed support reproducible research, open-data literacy, real-world analysis.

title: Convenient Access to Los Angeles Open Data API Endpoints
text: RSocrata package provides general interface Socrata-backed portal, laOpenData specifically tailored Los Angeles Open Data Portal. package part broader ecosystem tools working open data: nycOpenData — streamlined access NYC Open Data nysOpenData — streamlined access NY State Open Data mtaOpenData — streamlined access MTA-related NY State Open Data chiOpenData — streamlined access Chicago-related City Open Data Together, packages provide consistent, user-friendly interface working civic data across jurisdictions. Ease Use: need hunt 4x4 dataset IDs (e.g., 2ucp-7wg5); use catalog-based keys instead. Open Literacy: Designed specifically students researchers lower barrier entry civic data analysis.

what: laOpenData: Convenient Access to Los Angeles Open Data API Endpoints — laOpenData-package
title: laOpenData: Convenient Access to Los Angeles Open Data API Endpoints — laOpenData-package
text: Provides simple, reproducible access datasets Los Angeles Open Data portal https://data.lacity.org/. Functions return results tidy tibbles support optional filtering, sorting, row limits via Socrata API.

title: laOpenData: Convenient Access to Los Angeles Open Data API Endpoints — laOpenData-package
text: Maintainer: Christian Martinez c.martinez0@outlook.com (ORCID) (martinezc1)

what: Load Any Los Angeles Open Data Dataset — la_any_dataset
title: Load Any Los Angeles Open Data Dataset — la_any_dataset
text: Downloads Los Angeles Open Data dataset given Socrata JSON endpoint.

title: Load Any Los Angeles Open Data Dataset — la_any_dataset
code: la_any_dataset(   json_link,   limit = 10000,   timeout_sec = 30,   clean_names = TRUE,   coerce_types = TRUE )

title: Load Any Los Angeles Open Data Dataset — la_any_dataset
text: json_link Socrata dataset JSON endpoint URL (e.g., "https://data.lacity.org/resource/6rrh-rzua.json.json"). limit Number rows retrieve (default = 10,000). timeout_sec Request timeout seconds (default = 30). clean_names Logical; TRUE, convert column names snake_case (default = TRUE). coerce_types Logical; TRUE, attempt light type coercion (default = TRUE).

title: Load Any Los Angeles Open Data Dataset — la_any_dataset
text: tibble containing requested dataset.

title: Load Any Los Angeles Open Data Dataset — la_any_dataset
code: # Examples that hit the live Los Angeles Open Data API are guarded so CRAN checks # do not fail when the network is unavailable or slow. if (interactive() && curl::has_internet()) {   endpoint <- "https://data.lacity.org/resource/6rrh-rzua.json.json"   out <- try(la_any_dataset(endpoint, limit = 3), silent = TRUE)   if (!inherits(out, "try-error")) {     head(out)   } }

what: List datasets available in laOpenData — la_list_datasets
title: List datasets available in laOpenData — la_list_datasets
text: Retrieves current Open NY catalog returns datasets available use `la_pull_dataset()`.

title: List datasets available in laOpenData — la_list_datasets
List datasets available in laOpenData — la_list_datasets
text: tibble available datasets, including generated `key`, dataset   `id`, dataset `name`.

title: List datasets available in laOpenData — la_list_datasets
text: Keys generated dataset names using `janitor::make_clean_names()`.

title: List datasets available in laOpenData — la_list_datasets

what: Pull a Los Angeles Open Data dataset from the Los Angeles Open Data catalog — la_pull_dataset
title: Pull a Los Angeles Open Data dataset from the Los Angeles Open Data catalog — la_pull_dataset
text: Uses dataset `key` `id` `la_list_datasets()` pull data Los Angeles Open Data.

title: Pull a Los Angeles Open Data dataset from the Los Angeles Open Data catalog — la_pull_dataset
code: la_pull_dataset(   dataset,   limit = 10000,   filters = list(),   date = NULL,   from = NULL,   to = NULL,   date_field = NULL,   where = NULL,   order = NULL,   timeout_sec = 30,   clean_names = TRUE,   coerce_types = TRUE )

title: Pull a Los Angeles Open Data dataset from the Los Angeles Open Data catalog — la_pull_dataset
text: dataset dataset key ID `la_list_datasets()`. limit Number rows retrieve (default = 10,000). filters Optional named list filters. Supports vectors (translated ()). date Optional single date (matches times day) using `date_field`. Optional start date (inclusive) using `date_field`. Optional end date (exclusive) using `date_field`. date_field Optional date/datetime column use `date`, ``, ``. Must supplied `date`, ``, `` used. Optional raw SoQL clause. `date`, ``, `` provided, conditions -ed . order Optional SoQL ORDER clause. timeout_sec Request timeout seconds (default = 30). clean_names Logical; TRUE, convert column names snake_case (default = TRUE). coerce_types Logical; TRUE, attempt light type coercion (default = TRUE).

title: Pull a Los Angeles Open Data dataset from the Los Angeles Open Data catalog — la_pull_dataset
text: tibble.

title: Pull a Los Angeles Open Data dataset from the Los Angeles Open Data catalog — la_pull_dataset
text: Dataset keys generated dataset titles using `janitor::make_clean_names()`. keys derived live catalog metadata, dataset IDs stable option.

title: Pull a Los Angeles Open Data dataset from the Los Angeles Open Data catalog — la_pull_dataset
code: if (interactive() && curl::has_internet()) {   # Pull by key   la_pull_dataset("current_employee_names_salaries_and_position_titles", limit = 3)    # Pull by ID   la_pull_dataset("xzkq-xp2w", limit = 3)    # Filters   la_pull_dataset("xzkq-xp2w", limit = 3, filters = list(salary_or_hourly = "HOURLY"))  }
